title: Workplace Discipline
description: One wrong move in a disciplinary hearing and you're facing a CCMA reinstatement order — even when the employee was clearly guilty. South Africa's Labour Relations Act requires both substantive fairness (the employee is actually guilty) and procedural fairness (you followed the correct process). Get either wrong and the CCMA will overturn your dismissal, costing you reinstatement, back pay, or compensation. OptiHR manages discipline correctly: from charge sheets and hearings to CCMA representation.

whyOptiHR.title: Disciplinary hearings & charge sheets
whyOptiHR.subtitle: procedurally fair, legally defensible
whyOptiHR.body: We chair disciplinary hearings ensuring procedural fairness, draft clear charge sheets that comply with the Code of Good Practice and give employees adequate notice of allegations, conduct fair investigations into misconduct, and produce written outcomes that withstand CCMA scrutiny. We also advise on precautionary suspensions — ensuring they're lawful and don't trigger unfair labour practice claims.

whyOptiHR.title: CCMA representation & conciliation
whyOptiHR.subtitle: when disputes escalate
whyOptiHR.body: When dismissed employees refer unfair dismissal disputes to the CCMA, OptiHR represents your business in conciliation and arbitration. Our admitted attorney principal argues your case with legal authority — presenting evidence, cross-examining witnesses, and providing the level of representation that unions bring for employees.

whyOptiHR.title: Grievance handling & workplace mediation
whyOptiHR.subtitle: resolve conflicts before they escalate
whyOptiHR.body: We manage employee grievances fairly and promptly — preventing escalation to unfair labour practice disputes. We also mediate workplace conflicts between employees or between employees and management, resolving issues before they become formal disputes or toxic workplace situations that damage morale and productivity.

legalConsiderations.title: Code of Good Practice: Dismissal (2025)
legalConsiderations.subtitle: procedural and substantive fairness.
legalConsiderations.body: The 2025 Code of Good Practice governs dismissals for misconduct. It requires that employees are notified of charges, given opportunity to respond with a representative, and that the employer proves guilt on a balance of probabilities. The updated 2025 Code places stronger emphasis on both procedural correctness and substantive fairness.

legalConsiderations.title: Labour Relations Act — CCMA jurisdiction
legalConsiderations.subtitle: unfair dismissal and unfair labour practices.
legalConsiderations.body: Every dismissed employee has the right to refer an unfair dismissal dispute to the CCMA within 30 days of dismissal. The CCMA process involves conciliation, then arbitration if not resolved. Arbitration awards are enforceable as court orders. The cost of an unfair dismissal finding — reinstatement or up to 12 months' compensation — makes procedural compliance essential.

faq.question: Can we dismiss an employee for a first offence?
faq.answer: Yes, but only for serious misconduct — theft, assault, fraud, deliberate damage to property, or gross insubordination. For lesser misconduct, progressive discipline applies: verbal warning, written warning, final written warning, then dismissal. Dismissing for a first minor offence without following progressive discipline will likely be found procedurally or substantively unfair at the CCMA.

faq.question: What makes a dismissal procedurally unfair?
faq.answer: Common procedural failures include: insufficient notice of the hearing, failure to inform the employee of the charges in advance, not allowing a representative (union rep or fellow employee), conducting the hearing before the employee has had time to prepare, making a decision before the hearing, and failing to give the employee an opportunity to state their case. Any of these procedural failures can result in the CCMA overturning a dismissal — even where the employee was clearly guilty.

faq.question: What is a precautionary suspension and when can we use it?
faq.answer: A precautionary suspension removes an employee from the workplace pending investigation, typically used where the employee's presence would compromise the investigation, create safety risks, or allow destruction of evidence. It must be on full pay (unless the employment contract provides otherwise for serious misconduct). An unlawful or unpaid suspension can trigger an unfair labour practice dispute at the CCMA.

faq.question: What happens if we lose at the CCMA?
faq.answer: A CCMA arbitration award for unfair dismissal can order reinstatement (the employee returns to work as if never dismissed, including back pay) or compensation (up to 12 months' remuneration). Reinstatement orders can be enforced as court orders. Awards for unfair labour practices can also include compensation. This is why getting the process right from the start is essential.

faqBody: Questions we hear from employers about managing misconduct, disciplinary hearings, and CCMA disputes in South Africa.

sections.text: Disciplinary action isn't just about punishing bad behaviour—it's about following the law . South Africa's Labour Relations Act requires both substantive fairness (the employee is actually guilty) and procedural fairness (you followed the correct process) . Get either wrong and the CCMA will overturn your dismissal, costing you reinstatement, back pay, or compensation .
What we do:

Disciplinary Code & Policy Development — We create legally compliant disciplinary codes defining workplace rules, expected standards, and progressive disciplinary steps from verbal warnings to dismissal .
Disciplinary Hearing Management — We chair disciplinary hearings, ensuring procedural fairness, proper evidence presentation, and legally defensible outcomes .
Charge Sheet Drafting — We draft clear, specific charges that comply with the Code of Good Practice and give employees sufficient notice of allegations .
CCMA Representation — When disputes escalate to the CCMA, we represent you in conciliation and arbitration, presenting evidence and arguing your case .
Grievance Handling — We manage employee grievances fairly and promptly, preventing escalation to unfair labour practice disputes .
Workplace Mediation — We mediate workplace conflicts between employees, or between employees and management, resolving issues before they become formal disputes .
Precautionary Suspension — We advise on when and how to suspend employees pending investigation, ensuring the suspension is lawful and doesn't trigger unfair labour practice claims .
Investigation Support — We conduct fair, thorough workplace investigations into misconduct allegations, gathering evidence and interviewing witnesses .
Manager Training — We train your managers to handle disciplinary matters correctly, chair hearings, apply progressive discipline, and document everything properly .
What you get:
Legally compliant disciplinary policies and procedures for South Africa
Fair disciplinary hearings that hold up at the CCMA
Expert representation when disputes reach the CCMA or Labour Court
Quick, effective grievance resolution preventing costly escalation
Trained managers who know how to handle discipline legally
Protection from unfair dismissal claims and unfair labour practice disputes
Businesses in Gauteng and across South Africa facing misconduct and unsure how to handle it legally
Companies that have dismissed employees and now face CCMA unfair dismissal claims
Employers with no formal disciplinary code or outdated disciplinary procedures
Managers struggling to chair disciplinary hearings fairly and confidently
Organizations dealing with serious misconduct like theft, assault, insubordination, or dishonesty
Private schools managing educator discipline and SACE reporting requirements
Businesses with workplace conflicts, employee grievances, or toxic workplace issues
Companies needing CCMA representation for conciliation or arbitration
Legal authority you can trust — Our owner is an admitted attorney with Labour Court rights. We don't just advise on discipline—we represent you in the CCMA and Labour Court .
We chair hearings, not just advise — Unlike most HR consultants, we act as independent chairpersons for your disciplinary hearings, ensuring procedural fairness and legally defensible outcomes .
We know what the CCMA looks for — We've represented employers and employees at the CCMA. We know exactly what commissioners scrutinize and how to build bulletproof cases .
We handle the tough cases — Theft, fraud, assault, gross insubordination—we manage high-stakes disciplinary matters where the consequences of getting it wrong are severe .
We resolve disputes before they escalate — Through mediation and fair grievance handling, we solve workplace conflicts early, saving you time, money, and CCMA referrals .
We make discipline practical — We don't give you complicated policies you'll never use. We build systems that work in the real world and train your managers to use them .
